metadata.title: Claude, Vectorized.
metadata.description: line-art-vectorizer skill teardown: turn b+w line art into gate-passing SVGs. The skill tries and tosses what it can't do cleanly. A bad vector is worse than none.

beats.narration_text: Hallo, Liam. Today: line-art-vectorizer — named Dürer, for the master engraver. Two halves: generate SVG-friendly line art, then trace it through a gate. Try, and toss what can't be done cleanly. Liam, in for Bear.

props.runningText: * flatten → downscale → trace → judge → keep / toss

beats.narration_text: Two halves. Generate: a Midjourney prompt-set that produces SVG-friendly black-and-white art — bold, solid, thick lines. Convert: linework-to-svg.py flattens the source onto white, downscales, traces with vtracer, injects a viewBox, then judges each output. The gate has three toss buckets and one keep.

whatItDoes: Generate SVG-friendly art: Midjourney prompt-set in references/
Convert: linework-to-svg.py → vtracer → gate → KEEP / toss
Premium path: geometric icons authored from SVG primitives (beats tracing)
phaseGates: Generate: use Midjourney prompt-set for SVG-friendly b+w line art
Gate: svg-format-audit.mjs (viewBox, no raster, real paths)
Report: tried · kept · tossed (with per-image reason)

beats.narration_text: The loop: Midjourney prompt-set produces the black-and-white source. linework-to-svg.py flattens, downscales, traces with vtracer, injects viewBox. Then judges: keep if clean, toss with a reason if not. For geometric icons, skip the tracer and author from primitives. Audit every kept SVG with svg-format-audit.

steps.label: Generate art
steps.detail: Midjourney prompt-set → SVG-friendly b+w line art PNG

steps.label: Gate
steps.detail: KEEP (<40KB, >800B, valid) or toss with reason

steps.label: Audit
steps.detail: svg-format-audit.mjs: viewBox, no raster, real paths — hard gate

beats.narration_text: An atom icon. Trace it from a hand-drawn PNG — the result is twenty-nine kilobytes, one blob path, no groups, not animatable. Author it from primitives instead — ellipses and a circle. How big?

props.runningText: * tracing atom-sketch.png → checking size → 47KB > 40KB cap → TOSS
output: reason: sketch lines + detail → one blob, not an icon

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: hand-authored atom SVG from primitives — free, no trace. Three ellipses for orbits, one circle for nucleus. Nine hundred thirty-seven bytes. Grouped, animatable, audit-passing. The traced version was twenty-nine kilobytes and one blob path. The premium path wins: author what you can from primitives, trace what you must, toss what you can't do cleanly.

codeLines: <!-- SELF-DEMO: atom icon from SVG primitives (free — no trace) -->
<!-- group orbits, nucleus → animatable with CSS/JS -->
<!-- traced version: ~29KB blob path, one <path>, not animatable -->
captionLines: Premium path: geometric icons beat tracing by 30×.
Author from primitives. Trace organic art. Toss the rest.

beats.narration_text: Law one: a bad vector is worse than none. Simple art comes out clean; complex art is rejected with a reason, not faked. The gate exists because a 47-kilobyte blob path is not an icon — it's a liability. Tossing with a reason tells you what to fix upstream. Faking a passing vector tells you nothing and ships a problem.

props.quote: A bad vector is worse than none. Simple art comes out clean; complex art is rejected with a reason, not faked.
mechanismLines: Gate toss reasons: too complex (>40KB) · under-traced (<800B) · degenerate
Toss with reason = upstream fix signal
Fake pass = ships a problem silently
The honest limit is a feature, not a failure

beats.narration_text: Law two: don't expect simplify to manufacture simplicity that was never generated. The --simplify-auto flag escalates through blur and downscale levels. It reduces detail but doesn't redraw. A detailed character sketch is not an icon after aggressive simplification — it's a blob. Fix it upstream in the prompt-set, or recreate from primitives.

props.quote: Don't expect simplify to manufacture simplicity that was never generated.
mechanismLines: --simplify auto: escalates blur + downscale (levels 1-3), keeps first that fits
Reduces detail only — does not redraw or add structure
Colored, shaded, detailed illustrations → won't become clean icons
Fix upstream: use Midjourney prompt-set for solid/thick b+w art

beats.narration_text: Law three: never embed a raster inside an SVG — that's a fake vector and the gate fails it. The audit checks for embedded rasters, missing viewBox, and zero real paths. If the SVG wraps a PNG, it fails — even if it looks clean. The Madison cover mistake is the reason this gate exists. A fake vector ships a problem.

props.quote: Never embed a raster inside an `<svg>` — that's a fake vector and the gate fails it.
mechanismLines: svg-format-audit.mjs: viewBox present, no <image> raster, real <path> elements
Embedded raster = fake vector (file is SVG-wrapped PNG, not a path)
Gate fails it regardless of file size or appearance
The Madison cover mistake is the origin of this rule

beats.narration_text: Claude, Vectorized. The artifact is the gate decision: keep or toss, with a reason. Five kept, three tossed. The atom: hand-authored from ellipses and a circle, nine hundred thirty-seven bytes, audit-passing. Try, and toss what can't be done cleanly. A bad vector is worse than none.

artifactLines: → svg-format-audit.mjs (hard gate: viewBox, no raster, real paths)
deliveryLines: A bad vector is worse than none
Toss with reason · fix upstream · never fake a pass
Premium path: geometric icons 30× smaller than traced

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Generate your source art using the Midjourney prompt-set. Run the tracer. Check what the gate keeps and what it tosses. Author geometric icons from primitives. Liam, handing off to Bear.

props.runningText: * generate art → trace → gate → audit → deliver
output: generate: Midjourney prompt-set in references/ (SVG-friendly art)
gate: KEEP (<40KB, >800B) or toss with reason
geometric icons: author from SVG primitives instead

beats.narration_text: Claude, Vectorized. At NikBearBrown. Liam, in for Bear.
